← the Atlas

kolu Release Runbook

reference · seedling ·proposed ·JUN 06, 2026

Cut a release with `just release X.Y` — tag master, promote the Unreleased changelog entry, GitHub release. The changelog lives as a website content collection; the agent appends to it per PR, kolu.dev renders it Unreleased-and-all.

Contents

  • The changelog is website content
  • Keeping it filled — two checkpoints, no CI gate
  • kolu.dev renders it — Unreleased included
  • Cutting a release
  • Wiring (one-time, ships with this note’s PR)

kolu ships only as a Nix flake — users track master (nix run github:juspay/kolu). A release is a tag on master: a named point, a pin, a dated changelog entry. Nothing is published that master doesn’t already serve.

kolu is an application, not a library — no API to break, no downstream resolver reading the version. So the version is X.Y, picked by hand: a .0 is a milestone you’re calling ready, a .N bump is a normal release. No .Z (rolling, no maintenance branches — nothing to patch; you just ship the next minor). No SemVer contract, no computed bump.

The changelog is website content

It lives in the site, not a root CHANGELOG.md — a content collection beside the blog (blog collection — same shape). Each release is one entry; unreleased.mdx is the open one.

website/src/content/changelog/
  unreleased.mdx     ← agent appends here, every user-facing PR
  1-0.mdx            ← stamped { version: "1.0", date: 2026-06-06 } at release
---
version: Unreleased
---
### Added
- Compose surfaces as siblings — one canvas, no surface-app seams. ([#1201](https://github.com/juspay/kolu/pull/1201))
### Fixed
- Dock pings stop pulsing once you switch to the row. ([#1198](https://github.com/juspay/kolu/pull/1198))
### Heads-up
- `KOLU_STATE_DIR` moved under `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/kolu`; existing sessions auto-migrate.
  • Authored per PR by /be. Its implement step appends one line to unreleased.mdx under the right ### heading — full PR context, real prose, a markdown link to the PR. No conventional-commit prefixes, no bump field: kolu’s an app, nothing computes a version from them.
  • ### Heads-up is the editorial home for disruptive changes — removed feature, changed default, migration — written for the user.
  • Conflict-free. website/src/content/changelog/unreleased.mdx merge=union in .gitattributes auto-resolves concurrent appends — git keeps both lines, no marker, no rebase.

Keeping it filled — two checkpoints, no CI gate

A forgotten entry gets caught without a merge-blocking check, because there are already two human/agent checkpoints around it:

  • Write time — /be authors it. The changelog line is a step in /be’s implement phase (be §2 — Add a changelog entry). Every PR you cut goes through /be, so the entry rides along with the diff and the intent that produced it.
  • Publish time — the release prompt shows it. just release X.Y prints the exact Unreleased notes it’s about to publish and waits for y. A thin or empty section is visible at the only moment it matters — you’re staring at the release notes before they ship.

That’s enough for a single-maintainer, /be-driven repo. A merge-blocking changelog CI check (PR diff touches unreleased.mdx or carries a no-changelog label) only earns its keep once PRs arrive from contributors who don’t run /be — then you can’t trust the write-time step and want a hard gate. Add it then, not now.

kolu.dev renders it — Unreleased included

A /changelog page maps the collection newest-first, Unreleased at the top, so users see what shipped and what’s coming — and it’s your live preview: the notes are on the site the moment a PR merges, no dry-run needed.

Because the changelog is website/**, the existing Pages deploy already fires on every changelog edit (pages.yml — on.push.paths website/**) — no paths: change, no raw-import of a root file, nothing extra to wire.

Cutting a release

One command. It shows the plan — the version, the Unreleased notes it’s about to publish — and prompts before anything is committed or pushed. No --dry-run: the preview is the prompt, and answering n leaves the tree untouched.

release
$ just release 1.0
✓ preflight: master · clean · synced with origin · CI green
will stamp ## Unreleased → 1-0.mdx { version: 1.0, date: 2026-06-06 }
will bump version → 1.0, commit, tag v1.0, push, gh release
── notes to publish ──────────────────────────────
Added: Compose surfaces as siblings — one canvas, no seams.
Fixed: Dock pings stop pulsing once you switch to the row.
Heads-up: KOLU_STATE_DIR moved; sessions auto-migrate.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
Proceed? [y/N] y
→ committed release 1.0 + tag v1.0 · pushed · gh release created
✓ live: kolu.dev/changelog#v1-0 · github.com/juspay/kolu/releases/tag/v1.0
  • You pass X.Y — an editorial call, not a computed one.
  • Everything up to the prompt is read-only; the commit/tag/push happen only on y.
  • Preflight refuses to tag anything CI hasn’t already passed.
  • Any release is pinnable: nix run github:juspay/kolu/v1.0.

Wiring (one-time, ships with this note’s PR)

  • changelog collection — ✓ landed in #1208 : content.config.ts schema, the /changelog page, seeded unreleased.mdx, .gitattributes merge=union.
  • Keep-it-filled — the /be implement-step entry (.apm/skills/be/SKILL.md:36); no CI gate, the release prompt is the backstop. See Keeping it filled.
  • just release X.Y — the recipe above (stamp · bump · tag · push · gh release).
  • Identity rail — collapse the two version = "0.1.0" literals (default.nix:56, default.nix:101) into one let version = "0.1", stamp KOLU_VERSION beside KOLU_COMMIT_HASH (default.nix:203) → surface-app buildInfo cell → render 1.0 · a1b2c3d in the rail (IdentityRail srv column). Between releases it reads the last tag + commit: the series you’re on, plus the exact build.

Status: proposed. The changelog surface — the changelog collection, the /changelog page, the seeded 1.0 entry, and merge=union — landed in #1208 . Still to build: the just release X.Y recipe, the per-PR CI gate, and the KOLU_VERSION identity-rail stamp. First run: just release 1.0.
